architecture_summary: LXMERT is a Transformer-based cross-modality framework with three encoders: an object relationship encoder, a language encoder, and a cross-modality encoder. It learns vision-and-language connections through masked cross-modality language modeling, masked object prediction, cross-modality matching, and image question answering pre-training on large-scale image-and-sentence pairs.

benchmark_results.table_caption: Test-set results. VQA/GQA results are reported on the ‘test-standard’ splits and NLVR2 results are reported on the unreleased test set (‘Test-U’). The highest method results are in bold. Our LXMERT framework outperforms previous (comparable) state-of-the-art methods on all three datasets w.r.t. all metrics.

novel_contributions: a pre-trained vision-and-language cross-modality framework, LXMERT
three Transformer encoders: an object relationship encoder, a language encoder, and a cross-modality encoder
five diverse representative pre-training tasks for vision-and-language learning
a large aggregated pre-training dataset of 9.18M image-and-sentence pairs on 180K distinct images
state-of-the-art results on VQA and GQA, and a 22% absolute improvement on NLVR2

key_claims.claim: LXMERT is a pre-trained vision-and-language cross-modality framework built with three encoders and five pre-training tasks.
After fine-tuning, LXMERT achieves state-of-the-art results on VQA and GQA.
LXMERT improves the previous best result on NLVR2 by 22% absolute, from 54% to 76%.
The model uses self-attention and cross-attention layers to generate language, vision, and cross-modality representations.
Pre-training uses 9.18M image-and-sentence pairs from five vision-and-language datasets.
The whole pre-training process takes 10 days on 4 Titan Xp with batch size 256 for 20 epochs.
LXMERT outperforms previous (comparable) state-of-the-art methods on VQA, GQA, and NLVR2 test sets.
